title: Why the Strait of Hormuz is integral to Asia | Asia News Week | ABC News
description: 0:00 Why is the Strait of Hormuz integral to global economies? 0:55 How would China and Japan deal with restricted oil supply? 2:03 How will Asia's economies be impacted? 3:16 Is China dependent on Iranian oil?  In China, Japan, South Korea, Taiwan and India, leaders are focused on the Strait of Hormuz. About a fifth of the world's oil output and liquefied natural gas is shipped through the narrow body of water, wedged between Iran and Oman, but traffic through the passage has slowed to a trickle after the outbreak of war. Kpler analyst, Muyu Xu explains to Asia News Week’s Yvonne Yong why the developments could see Asia’s energy security at risk. title: Trump demands 'unconditional surrender' from Iran as strikes intensify | ABC NEWS
description: Donald Trump says there will be no deal with Iran except "unconditional surrender" as the war in the Middle East enters its second week. His post on Truth Social came hours after Iran's president claimed mediation efforts were underway. The UN refugee agency says the conflict between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon is a "major humanitarian emergency". title: How will the Middle East war affect Australia's economy? | ABC NEWS
description: Concerns are growing worldwide about how the Iranian conflict could affect the global economy, as Qatar's energy minister warned that the Gulf could stop oil production within days if ships cannot proceed through the Strait of Hormuz. Justin Wolfers, professor of economics at the University of Michigan, says Australians are particularly at risk as we import more oil than we export. title: Kids taught to shoot with illegal weapons in PNG’s tribal warfare | ABC NEWS
description: UN-sponsored research has found that some of the most dangerous weapons in PNG have been trafficked by soldiers or police and sold on the black market. Politicians and powerful elites are often involved in purchasing these weapons. A draft report says these elites buy illegal guns for remote tribes to protect land, mining interests and political seats. The ABC has been told that children as young as eight are being trained to use the weapons and that women are being trafficked. description: 00:00 US President Donald Trump says there will be "no deal with Iran except unconditional surrender", as the conflict in the Middle East enters its second week. title: Significant flooding across the Top End sees hundreds evacuated and Katherine inundated | ABC NEWS
description: 00:00 Floodwater has entered parts of the Katherine town centre and overwhelmed the town's levee system. Major flooding from the Katherine River is inundating parts of the town and the water level is expected to peak at 19.2 metres on Saturday night. Significant water has risen around the visitor centre and shops, with water rushing in from the direction of the levee, which has held back a large body of water for most of the day. 02:00 Hundreds of remote residents have been evacuated from their small communities by air this weekend, as flooding inundates parts of the Northern Territory. The Bureau of Meteorology (BOM) has a number of flood warnings in place, including an emergency alert for major flooding in Katherine and a major flood warning for the Daly River. The weather bureau said major flooding is expected at the Daly River Police Station from Saturday evening, with levels expected to reach 14.5 metres on Sunday. By James Elton and Roxanne Fitzgerald for ABC News Northern Territory. title: Counting underway in the Nightcliff by-election in Darwin | ABC NEWS
description: Vote counting has started in Nightcliff, with the Greens trying to hold on to the marginal seat it won from Labor by just 36 votes in the 2024 Northern Territory election. The by-election was triggered in February by the resignation of Greens politician Kat McNamara for health reasons. Ms McNamara became the first Green to win a seat in the NT Parliament when she claimed Nightcliff by just 36 votes in the 2024 general election. Four candidates are vying to replace her, including Suki Dorras-Walker from the Greens, independent Phil Scott, Labor's Ed Smelt and Anjan Paudel from the CLP. By Jack Hislop for ABC News Northern Territory.
